title_en: Introduction to Al-Muhalla bil-Athar by Ibn Hazm
content_en:
Al-Muhalla bil-Athar ('The Adorned with Transmitted Reports') is the most comprehensive work of Dhahiri (Literalist) jurisprudence ever written, authored by the brilliant and controversial Andalusian polymath Abu Muhammad 'Ali ibn Ahmad ibn Sa'id ibn Hazm al-Andalusi al-Qurtubi (384–456 AH / 994–1064 CE). It represents both the pinnacle of the Dhahiri school and one of the most exhaustive works of comparative Islamic jurisprudence ever produced.

Ibn Hazm was born in Cordoba, the capital of Andalusian Islamic civilization at its height, to a prominent family in the Umayyad administration. His early education was literary and philosophical, but he turned to Islamic jurisprudence and theology in his mature years, adopting and developing the Dhahiri school founded by Dawud ibn Khalaf al-Dhahiri (d. 270 AH). Ibn Hazm became the school's most formidable champion, defending it with intellectual brilliance and polemical sharpness that earned him both devoted admirers and fierce critics.

The Dhahiri school (from dhahir — the apparent, outward meaning) holds that Islamic law is derived exclusively from the Quran, the authentic Sunnah, and the explicit consensus of the Companions — and that analogical reasoning (qiyas) is not a valid source of law. This rejection of qiyas distinguishes the Dhahiri school from all four Sunni schools, which accept it as a fundamental tool of legal reasoning. Ibn Hazm's rejection of qiyas was not based on laziness but on a principled conviction that extending the law through human analogy opened the door to arbitrary legal creativity that violated the principle of divine sovereignty in lawmaking.

Al-Muhalla is organized by legal chapters in the standard fiqh arrangement. For each ruling, Ibn Hazm states his position, presents the evidence (primarily hadiths, which he analyzes with the rigor of a hadith specialist), and then critiques the positions of the other schools — particularly the Maliki school dominant in Andalusia — with characteristic directness. His critiques are sometimes brilliant and sometimes overly sharp, but they consistently model the approach of returning every ruling to its textual foundation.

The scholarly community's relationship to Ibn Hazm is complex. He is unanimously recognized as a genius of extraordinary range — his works on polemics, theology, literary theory, ethics, and comparative religion (al-Fasl fi al-Milal) attest to an encyclopedic mind. His fiqh methodology has influenced later scholars of all schools who emphasized strict hadith-based reasoning, including Ibn Taymiyya and ash-Shawkani. Al-Muhalla remains an essential reference in Islamic jurisprudence for its comprehensive analysis of the hadith evidence behind legal rulings, even for scholars who reject the Dhahiri approach.

The Dhahiri positions in Al-Muhalla are presented here as a legitimate scholarly tradition within Islamic legal discourse, representing the approach of a significant minority of classical Muslim scholars who emphasized strict textual fidelity in legal derivation.
title_id: Pengantar Al-Muhalla bil-Athar Karya Ibn Hazm
content_id:
Al-Muhalla bil-Athar ('Yang Dihiasi dengan Riwayat yang Dinukil') adalah karya fikih Dhahiri (Literalis) paling komprehensif yang pernah ditulis, karangan polimat Andalusia yang brilian dan kontroversial, Abu Muhammad 'Ali ibn Ahmad ibn Sa'id ibn Hazm al-Andalusi al-Qurtubi (384-456 H / 994-1064 M). Karya ini merupakan puncak mazhab Dhahiri sekaligus salah satu karya fikih Islam komparatif paling menyeluruh yang pernah diproduksi.

Ibn Hazm lahir di Cordoba, ibu kota peradaban Islam Andalusia pada puncaknya, dari keluarga terkemuka dalam administrasi Umayyah. Pendidikan awalnya bersifat sastra dan filosofis, namun ia beralih ke fikih dan teologi Islam pada masa dewasanya, mengadopsi dan mengembangkan mazhab Dhahiri yang didirikan oleh Dawud ibn Khalaf al-Dhahiri (w. 270 H). Ibn Hazm menjadi juara mazhab yang paling tangguh, membelanya dengan kecemerlangan intelektual dan ketajaman polemik yang membuatnya mendapat pengagum setia sekaligus pengkritik keras.

Mazhab Dhahiri (dari kata dhahir - makna yang tampak, lahiriah) menyatakan bahwa hukum Islam diturunkan secara eksklusif dari Alquran, Sunnah yang autentik, dan ijma' Sahabat yang eksplisit - dan bahwa penalaran analogis (qiyas) bukan merupakan sumber hukum yang valid. Penolakan terhadap qiyas ini membedakan mazhab Dhahiri dari keempat mazhab Sunni, yang menerimanya sebagai alat fundamental penalaran hukum. Penolakan Ibn Hazm terhadap qiyas tidak didasarkan pada kemalasan, melainkan pada keyakinan prinsipil bahwa memperluas hukum melalui analogi manusia membuka pintu kreativitas hukum yang sewenang-wenang, yang melanggar prinsip kedaulatan ilahi dalam pembuatan hukum.

Al-Muhalla disusun berdasarkan bab-bab hukum dalam susunan fikih standar. Untuk setiap hukum, Ibn Hazm menyatakan posisinya, menyajikan dalil (terutama hadis, yang ia analisis dengan ketelitian seorang spesialis hadis), kemudian mengkritik posisi mazhab-mazhab lain - terutama mazhab Maliki yang dominan di Andalusia - dengan ketegasan yang khas. Kritik-kritiknya kadang brilian dan kadang terlalu tajam, tetapi secara konsisten memodelkan pendekatan untuk mengembalikan setiap hukum ke fondasi tekstualnya.

Hubungan komunitas ilmiah dengan Ibn Hazm tidaklah sederhana. Ia diakui secara bulat sebagai seorang jenius dengan jangkauan luar biasa - karya-karyanya tentang polemik, teologi, teori sastra, etika, dan agama komparatif (al-Fasl fi al-Milal) membuktikan pikiran yang ensiklopedis. Metodologi fikihnya telah mempengaruhi ulama kemudian dari semua mazhab yang menekankan penalaran berbasis hadis yang ketat, termasuk Ibn Taimiyyah dan asy-Syawkani. Al-Muhalla tetap menjadi referensi penting dalam fikih Islam untuk analisis komprehensif tentang dalil hadis di balik hukum-hukum syariat, bahkan bagi ulama yang menolak pendekatan Dhahiri.

Posisi-posisi Dhahiri dalam Al-Muhalla disajikan di sini sebagai tradisi ilmiah yang sah dalam wacana hukum Islam, mewakili pendekatan sebagian kecil ulama Muslim klasik yang menekankan kesetiaan tekstual yang ketat dalam derivasi hukum.
content_ar: Al-Muhalla bil-Athar ('The Adorned with Transmitted Reports') is the most comprehensive work of Dhahiri (Literalist) jurisprudence ever written, authored by the brilliant and controversial Andalusian polymath Abu محمد ﷺ 'علي ibn أحمد ibn Sa'id ibn Hazm al-Andalusi al-Qurtubi (384–456 AH / 994–1064 CE). It represents both the pinnacle of the Dhahiri school and one of the most exhaustive works of comparative Islamic jurisprudence ever produced. Ibn Hazm was born in Cordoba, the capital of Andalusian Islamic civilization at its height, to a prominent الأسرة in the Umayyad administration. His early education was literary and philosophical, but he turned to Islamic jurisprudence and theology in his mature years, adopting and developing the Dhahiri school founded by داود ﷺ ibn Khalaf al-Dhahiri (d. 270 AH). Ibn Hazm became the school's most formidable champion, defending it with intellectual brilliance and polemical sharpness that earned him both devoted admirers and fierce critics. The Dhahiri school (from dhahir — the apparent, outward meaning) holds that Islamic law is derived exclusively from the القرآن الكريم, the authentic السنة, and the explicit consensus of the الصحابة — and that analogical reasoning (القياس) is not a valid source of law. This rejection of القياس distinguishes the Dhahiri school from all four Sunni schools, which accept it as a الأساسي tool of legal reasoning. Ibn Hazm's rejection of القياس was not based on laziness but on a principled conviction that extending the law through human analogy opened the door to arbitrary legal creativity that violated the principle of divine sovereignty in lawmaking. Al-Muhalla is organized by legal الأبواب in the standard الفقه arrangement. For each ruling, Ibn Hazm states his position, presents the evidence (primarily hadiths, which he analyzes with the rigor of a الحديث specialist), and then critiques the positions of the other schools — particularly the المالكي school dominant in Andalusia — with characteristic directness. His critiques are sometimes brilliant and sometimes overly sharp, but they consistently model the approach of returning every ruling to its textual foundation. The العلمي الجماعة's relationship to Ibn Hazm is complex. He is unanimously recognized as a genius of extraordinary range — his works on polemics, theology, literary theory, ethics, and comparative religion (al-Fasl fi al-Milal) attest to an encyclopedic العقل. His الفقه methodology has influenced later العلماء of all schools who emphasized strict الحديث-based reasoning, بما في ذلك Ibn Taymiyya and ash-Shawkani. Al-Muhalla remains an الجوهري reference in Islamic jurisprudence for its comprehensive analysis of the الحديث evidence behind legal rulings, even for العلماء who reject the Dhahiri approach. The Dhahiri positions in Al-Muhalla are presented here as a legitimate العلمي التراث within Islamic legal discourse, representing the approach of a المهم minority of c
